regimen.name: GnP-MPACT
regimen.description: Gemcitabine 1000 mg/m^2 + nab-paclitaxel 125 mg/m^2 IV on days 1, 8, 15 of a 28-day cycle (Von Hoff 2013, MPACT). Curves are population priors; blend with personal data via n_effective.

encoding_notes.expected_mean: Population mean for the metric on that cycle day. Units are per-metric; see each metric's `unit` field.
encoding_notes.expected_sd: Standard deviation across the population on that cycle day. Encodes between-patient + within-patient variance unless stated otherwise.
encoding_notes.n_effective: Prior strength as effective sample size. 4-5 = strong literature; 2-3 = moderate / extrapolated from analogous regimens; 1 = weak / inferred. The platform should let personal data dominate within ~n_effective observations.
encoding_notes.missing_data: If `expected_mean: null`, no published curve exists at this resolution; the field is shipped as a placeholder so the personal-data engine can populate it from observation.

anc.summary: Gemcitabine-driven neutropenia with ~3-day onset and ~3-day recovery from a single dose; with day 1, 8, 15 dosing the population nadir falls in days 15-21 (after the 3rd weekly dose). Day 8 ANC is the operational gate for the day-8 dose decision.
anc.notes: Mean baseline ANC ~3.5-4.5 x10^9/L (PDAC patients). Assumes no growth-factor support. ~38-67% of cycles show grade >=3 (ANC <1.0); grade 4 (ANC <0.5) ~10-15%. SDs are wide — between-patient variance dominates. After cycle 3, baseline (day 1) ANC drifts ~0.3-0.5 x10^9/L lower (cumulative marrow effect, not encoded here — handled by cycle-number adjustment).

hemoglobin.summary: Anemia is near-universal (98% any-grade in MPACT) but with comparatively flat within-cycle dynamics. The dominant signal is a slow downward drift across cycles, not a within-cycle nadir. Within a single cycle, Hb falls modestly between days 8-21 then partially recovers by day 28, but this effect is small (~0.3-0.6 g/dL) compared with the ~1.0-1.5 g/dL drop seen across cycles 1->4. SDs reflect substantial between-patient variability tied to baseline Hb and transfusion practice.
hemoglobin.notes: Baseline (cycle 1, day 1) ~12.0 g/dL is typical for PDAC patients meeting MPACT eligibility (>=9 g/dL). Within-cycle curve is encoded with HIGH SD (n_effective=2) because no high-resolution daily Hb curve has been published; values are consistent with general gemcitabine pharmacology and clinical experience but should be considered weak priors.

platelets.summary: Gemcitabine is among the most thrombocytopenia-inducing cytotoxics. With weekly dosing the platelet nadir falls around day 14-15 of a 21-28-day cycle and recovers by day 21-28. Grade >=3 thrombocytopenia (<50) ~13% per MPACT; any-grade ~74-83%.
platelets.notes: Baseline platelets ~250 x10^9/L (PDAC eligibility floor 100 x10^9/L). Within-cycle curve well-supported by gemcitabine literature broadly (n_effective=3); exact nadir day shows substantial inter-patient variance.

fatigue_proctcae.summary: Population-level fatigue follows a within-week 'pulse' pattern after each weekly infusion: rises days 2-3, peaks days 3-5 (overlapping ANC nadir for the day-15 dose, peaking day 18-20), partially recovers days 6-7 before the next dose. Across cycles, baseline fatigue drifts upward modestly. No published high-resolution PRO-CTCAE day-by-day curve in PDAC GnP exists; the curve is extrapolated from taxane fatigue kinetics + ESAS pancreatic data.
fatigue_proctcae.notes: Encoded as PRO-CTCAE severity 0-4 (None/Mild/Moderate/Severe/VerySevere). Baseline expected ~1.2 (mild) for treated PDAC patient. Grade 3-4 fatigue 17% in MPACT (point prevalence). SD wide — fatigue is highly individual.

nausea_proctcae.summary: Acute nausea peaks 6-24h post infusion; delayed nausea peaks 48-72h. Gemcitabine is low-emetogenic (10-30% baseline); paclitaxel acute pain syndrome can extend GI symptoms days 2-4. Each weekly dose triggers a small repeating peak. By day 22-28 (rest week) nausea mostly returns to pre-cycle baseline.
nausea_proctcae.notes: Steroid premed (dex) blunts day 1-2 peak. Encoded population mean reflects modern triplet antiemetic prophylaxis (5HT3 + dex; NK1 not routine for low-emetogenic gemcitabine). Grade 3 nausea ~6% MPACT.

neuropathy_proctcae_within_cycle.summary: Within a single cycle, neuropathy severity is RELATIVELY FLAT — it does not pulse with weekly doses the way fatigue/nausea do. The dominant trajectory is across-cycle accumulation. We model within-cycle as constant + small day 2-3 increments after each infusion (acute neuropathic 'flare' from cold dysaesthesia + paclitaxel acute pain syndrome).
neuropathy_proctcae_within_cycle.notes: Use neuropathy_cumulative_by_cycle for the axis-3 toxicity model. The within-cycle field is included for completeness but n_effective=1 — personal data should dominate quickly.

curve.note: Use cumulative-by-cycle prior; within-cycle daily curve not described in literature.

neuropathy_cumulative_by_cycle.metric: Cumulative peripheral neuropathy grade by cycle number (axis-3 toxicity)
neuropathy_cumulative_by_cycle.summary: THIS IS THE CRITICAL AXIS-3 CURVE FOR THE BRIDGE STRATEGY. Median time to grade 3 neuropathy in MPACT = 140 days (~5 cycles). nab-paclitaxel cumulative dose is the dominant predictor. Grade >=2 typically appears between cycles 3-5; grade 3 between cycles 5-7. Reversibility: median time from grade 3 -> grade 1 = 29 days.
neuropathy_cumulative_by_cycle.notes: Encoded as cycle-number based mean cumulative grade. Curve assumes a patient who completes scheduled doses without dose reduction. Dose reductions / function-preserving regimens (which Hu Lin is on) shift the curve right by 1-2 cycles. Personal trajectory should override population priors quickly because individual neuropathy susceptibility (CYP2C8 genotype, baseline, age, diabetes) drives huge variance.

curve.note: Most MPACT patients had stopped or dose-reduced by cycle 9; data sparse.
Plateau is an artifact of selection — patients with worsening neuropathy stop/reduce.

weight.summary: Cycle-day weight kinetics within a single 28-day GnP cycle are NOT described in the literature at daily resolution. The dominant signal is cycle-over-cycle drift driven by cachexia. Mean weight change in PDAC GnP cohorts: -1 to -3 kg per cycle in the cachexia-vulnerable subgroup; weight-stable or slight gain in well-supported patients. Within-cycle, transient ~0.5-1.5 kg weight loss occurs days 2-5 (anorexia + nausea), recovers by days 8-14.
weight.notes: Encoded as kg change from cycle day 1 weight. Across-cycle drift handled separately. n_effective=1 for the within-cycle daily curve because it is extrapolated from general post-chemo anorexia patterns, not from PDAC-specific daily-weight data.

albumin.summary: Albumin shows slow downward drift across cycles, not a within-cycle curve. A >=10% decrease at week 4 (i.e. by start of cycle 2) predicts worse OS in MPACT-style cohorts. Daily within-cycle dynamics are not described. Baseline ~38 g/L typical for treated PDAC.
albumin.notes: Encoded with null daily means; populate from personal data. across_cycle_drift is the load-bearing prior.

ecog_self_rated.metric: Self-rated ECOG performance status by cycle day
ecog_self_rated.summary: NO PUBLISHED DAY-BY-DAY ECOG SELF-RATING CURVE EXISTS for GnP-treated PDAC. ECOG is conventionally assessed by clinician at each clinic visit (~weekly during dosing), not by patient daily. Self-rated ECOG correlates moderately with clinician ECOG (kappa 0.4-0.6) but tends to track the within-week fatigue pulse. The platform should treat self-rated ECOG as a derived/inferred metric from fatigue + functional self-assessment, not as a primary input with a population prior.
ecog_self_rated.notes: All cycle days null. n_effective=1 across the board. Personal data should populate this within ~7-14 observations.

curve.note: No published curve. Patient-rated ECOG is not a standard PRO.

ca199.metric: CA 19-9 measurement cadence and biological variation
ca199.summary: CA 19-9 is NOT a daily/cycle-day metric. Standard practice is q4-8 weeks (NCCN: every 1-3 months during active treatment). Intra-individual biological CV in healthy individuals = 27.2% (Erden 2008); reference change value (RCV, p<0.05, unidirectional) = 64.7%. In treated cancer this CV is likely larger because biliary/inflammatory confounders fluctuate. Practical rule: a single inter-measurement change <~30-35% is within biological noise; >65-100% is biologically meaningful.
ca199.notes: Encoded as a CADENCE + variation prior, not a daily curve. The platform should: (a) suggest measurements every 28-56 days (i.e. cycle 1 day 1, then ~q2 cycles), (b) require >=2-3 consecutive rises to yellow-flag the disease axis (already encoded in thresholds.json: ca199_rising_consecutive_yellow=3), (c) use 35% as the within-noise band and 65% as the meaningful-change band when comparing two values.

cadence.rationale: Aligned to GnP cycle length so CA19-9 is drawn day 1 of each new cycle alongside scheduled bloods.

biological_variation.source_population: healthy individuals; assume CV is at least as large in PDAC patients with biliary involvement

confounders: biliary obstruction / cholestasis (can elevate CA19-9 acutely)
Lewis-negative phenotype (~5-10% non-secretors produce no CA19-9)

citations.vonhoff_nejm_2013: Von Hoff DD, Ervin T, Arena FP, et al. Increased Survival in Pancreatic Cancer with nab-Paclitaxel plus Gemcitabine. N Engl J Med. 2013;369(18):1691-1703. doi:10.1056/NEJMoa1304369. PMID 24131140.
citations.mpact_supplement: MPACT Trial Supplementary Appendix. Von Hoff DD, et al. NEJM 2013, supplementary materials (cycle-by-cycle dose modifications, AE timing tables).
citations.scheithauer_jgo_2016: Scheithauer W, Ramanathan RK, Moore M, et al. Dose modification and efficacy of nab-paclitaxel plus gemcitabine vs. gemcitabine for patients with metastatic pancreatic cancer: phase III MPACT trial. J Gastrointest Oncol. 2016;7(3):469-478. doi:10.21037/jgo.2016.01.04. PMID 27284481.
citations.goldstein_jnci_2015: Goldstein D, El-Maraghi RH, Hammel P, et al. nab-Paclitaxel plus gemcitabine for metastatic pancreatic cancer: long-term survival from a phase III trial. JNCI. 2015;107(2):dju413. doi:10.1093/jnci/dju413.
citations.andre_neuropathy_2016: Reni M, Wan Y, Solem C, et al. Development of peripheral neuropathy and its association with survival during treatment with nab-paclitaxel plus gemcitabine for patients with metastatic adenocarcinoma of the pancreas: A subset analysis from a randomised phase III trial (MPACT). Eur J Cancer. 2016;57:10-20. PMID 26655559. (Median time to grade 3 PN 140 days; recovery G3->G1 29 days.)
citations.kang_jcancer_2024: Kang JS, et al. Factors affecting peripheral neuropathy induced by nanoparticle albumin-bound paclitaxel in patients with pancreatic cancer. Br J Clin Pharmacol. 2024;90(11). doi:10.1111/bcp.16210.
citations.kang_jcancer_2025: Factors Affecting Delayed Recovery from Neutropenia in Patients with Pancreatic Cancer Receiving Gemcitabine plus Nab-Paclitaxel. J Cancer. 2025;16:1413.
citations.kareva_pkpd_2014: Schmitt A, et al. Dose Schedule Optimization and the Pharmacokinetic Driver of Neutropenia. PLOS One. 2014. (16-day moving-average exposure window predicts ANC nadir.)
citations.soff_2017_eltrombopag: Soff GA, et al. Eltrombopag for thrombocytopenia in patients with advanced solid tumors receiving gemcitabine-based chemotherapy: a randomized, placebo-controlled phase 2 study. 2017. PMID 28864871. (Time-to-platelet recovery 8 vs 15 days.)
citations.berger_fatigue_2007: Berger AM, et al. Course of fatigue between two cycles of adjuvant chemotherapy in breast cancer patients. 2007. PMID 17135820. (Fatigue peaks days 3-5 after taxane.)
citations.esas_pancreas_2019: Symptom trajectories and predictors of severe symptoms in pancreatic cancer (ESAS data). HPB. 2019.
citations.esmo_fatigue_2020: Fabi A, et al. Cancer-related fatigue: ESMO Clinical Practice Guidelines for diagnosis and treatment. Ann Oncol. 2020;31(6):713-723.
citations.navari_cinv_2016: Navari RM, Aapro M. Antiemetic Prophylaxis for Chemotherapy-Induced Nausea and Vomiting. N Engl J Med. 2016;374(14):1356-1367.
citations.rapoport_cinv_2017: Rapoport BL. Delayed Chemotherapy-Induced Nausea and Vomiting: Pathogenesis, Incidence, and Current Management. Front Pharmacol. 2017. (Delayed CINV peaks 48-72h.)
citations.nccn_antiemesis_2026: NCCN Clinical Practice Guidelines in Oncology: Antiemesis. v.1.2026.
citations.nccn_pdac_2026: NCCN Clinical Practice Guidelines in Oncology: Pancreatic Adenocarcinoma. v.1.2026. (CA19-9 monitoring q1-3 months on active treatment.)
citations.hertz_calgb40502_2018: Hertz DL, et al. Kinetic-Pharmacodynamic Model of Chemotherapy-Induced Peripheral Neuropathy in Patients with Metastatic Breast Cancer Treated with Paclitaxel, Nab-Paclitaxel, or Ixabepilone: CALGB 40502 (Alliance). AAPS J. 2018;20:24. PMC5711539.
citations.bachmann_cachexia_2009: Bachmann J, Heiligensetzer M, Krakowski-Roosen H, Büchler MW, Friess H, Martignoni ME. Cachexia worsens prognosis in patients with resectable pancreatic cancer. J Gastrointest Surg. 2008;12(7):1193-1201. (Cited by Bachmann 2009 reviews on cachexia and immobility.)
citations.fearon_consensus_2011: Fearon K, Strasser F, Anker SD, et al. Definition and classification of cancer cachexia: an international consensus. Lancet Oncol. 2011;12(5):489-495.
citations.bilen_jco_2024_tocilizumab: Bilen MA, et al. Randomized Phase II Study of Nab-Paclitaxel and Gemcitabine With or Without Tocilizumab as First-Line Treatment in Advanced Pancreatic Cancer: Survival and Cachexia. J Clin Oncol. 2024. (Body-weight changes at baseline, 2 mo, 4 mo on GnP.)
citations.naumann_2021_loss: Naumann P, et al. Loss of adipose tissue or skeletal muscle during first-line gemcitabine/nab-paclitaxel therapy is associated with worse survival. 2021. PMID 34818466.
citations.macarulla_albumin_2023: Macarulla T, et al. Clinical and biological markers predictive of treatment response associated with metastatic pancreatic adenocarcinoma. Br J Cancer. 2023. (>=10% albumin drop at 4wk -> HR 4.75 for OS.)
citations.sasaki_albumin_2018: Sasaki T, et al. Serum albumin <3.5 g/dL is a poor prognostic factor in advanced PDAC on gemcitabine-based therapy. (HR 1.70.)
citations.erden_biological_variation_2008: Erden G, Barazi AO, Tezcan G, Yildirimkaya MM. Biological variation and reference change values of CA 19-9, CEA, AFP in serum of healthy individuals. Scand J Clin Lab Invest. 2008;68(3):212-218. PMID 17926198. (CV_intra 27.2%, RCV 64.7%.)
citations.ballehaninna_review_2012: Ballehaninna UK, Chamberlain RS. The clinical utility of serum CA 19-9 in the diagnosis, prognosis and management of pancreatic adenocarcinoma: An evidence based appraisal. J Gastrointest Oncol. 2012;3(2):105-119.
citations.westgard_bv_database: Ricos C, et al. Biological variation database (Westgard QC). Updated annually.
citations.ecog_oken_1982: Oken MM, Creech RH, Tormey DC, et al. Toxicity and response criteria of the Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group. Am J Clin Oncol. 1982;5(6):649-655.
citations.blagden_2003_ecog_agreement: Blagden SP, Charman SC, Sharples LD, Magee LR, Gilligan D. Performance status score: do patients and their oncologists agree? Br J Cancer. 2003;89(6):1022-1027. (Patient vs clinician ECOG kappa.)
citations.ctcae_v5: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services, NCI. Common Terminology Criteria for Adverse Events (CTCAE) v5.0. November 2017.
